originalTitle: Homes evacuated as firefighters tackle railway embankment blaze in east London
summary: Fire in Walthamstow affected one house and multiple gardens and sheds, according to London fire brigade
content: Residents have been evacuated from their homes after a fire at a railway embankment in east London. Twenty fire engines and about 125 firefighters were called to the incident near Vallentin Road in Walthamstow. The London fire brigade (LFB) said the fire affected one house and multiple gardens and sheds, as well as the railway embankment. No injuries were reported. The incident was due to an electrical fire caused by a fallen tree, the Guardian understands. The LFB said the blaze was producing a “significant amount of smoke” and people in the area were advised to close their windows and doors, while motorists have been urged to avoid the area, including Wood Street. The Weaver Overground line has been partly closed. A 32-metre turntable ladder was also in attendance and has been deployed as a water tower to help extinguish the flames from above, while the brigade’s drone team were also at the scene. The LFB assistant commissioner, Tom Goodall, said: “We deployed a significant amount of resources to this fire and firefighters worked in hot and arduous conditions to bring it under control as swiftly as possible. “One house was alight, and a second home has suffered damage to a rear extension. The gardens of around 30 properties have also unfortunately been affected by the fire. However, crews worked extremely hard to prevent the fire from spreading to further homes. “A railway embankment was also alight and this has caused disruption to the Weaver London Overground line … The brigade will retain a significant presence on the scene through the night, working to fully extinguish the fire.” The LFB said it received the first of more than 100 calls at 6.27pm and control officers have mobilised crews from Tottenham, Bethnal Green, Walthamstow, Chingford and surrounding fire stations to the scene. Stella Creasy, the local MP, posted a video from the scene on Instagram. She said: “Please avoid the Wood Street area. We need to be able to support the families who are being evacuated. “If you can help with refreshments please go to St Mary’s Welcome Centre, the team there would appreciate any offers of food and water.” In a subsequent video, Creasy said most of the evacuated residents in surrounding roads can return to their homes. Another rest centre has been set up at Walthamstow Central library for people affected in partnership with Waltham Forest council.
chineseTitle: 消防队员扑灭伦敦东部铁路路堤大火，房屋被疏散
chineseSummary: 据伦敦消防队称，沃尔瑟姆斯托的火灾影响了一所房屋以及多个花园和棚屋
chineseContent: 伦敦东部的铁路路堤发生火灾后，居民已从家中疏散。 20 辆消防车和约 125 名消防员被召集到沃尔瑟姆斯托瓦伦丁路附近的事故现场。伦敦消防队（LFB）表示，火灾影响到一栋房屋、多个花园和棚屋，以及铁路路堤。没有人员受伤的报告。据《卫报》了解，这起事件是由倒下的树木引起的电气火灾造成的。 LFB表示，大火产生了“大量烟雾”，建议该地区的人们关闭门窗，同时敦促驾车者避开该地区，包括伍德街。 Weaver 地上铁线已部分关闭。一架 32 米长的转盘梯也出现在现场，并被部署为水塔，以帮助扑灭上方的火焰，而该旅的无人机小组也在现场。 LFB助理专员汤姆·古道尔(Tom Goodall)表示：“我们为这场火灾部署了大量资源，消防队员在炎热和艰苦的条件下工作，以尽快控制火势。”一栋房屋起火，第二栋房屋的后部扩建部分受损。不幸的是，大约 30 处房产的花园也受到了火灾的影响。然而，工作人员非常努力地防止火势蔓延到其他房屋。 “一条铁路路堤也着火了，这对韦弗伦敦地上铁线造成了干扰……该旅将在夜间在现场保留大量人员，努力完全扑灭大火。” LFB 表示，下午 6 点 27 分接到了 100 多个电话中的第一个，控制人员已动员来自托特纳姆、贝斯纳尔格林、沃尔瑟姆斯托、清福德和周边消防站的人员。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
